channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: From side gigs to canceling trips, Americans adjust to months of high gas prices
item.description: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.content:encoded: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Social Security funds could run short by 2032, program's Trustees warn
item.description: A trust fund that helps to finance Social Security benefits is expected to run out of money in less than seven years — unless Congress acts to patch the system before that.
item.content:encoded:
A trust fund that helps to finance Social Security benefits is expected to run out of money in less than seven years — unless Congress acts to patch the system before that.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: From side gigs to canceling trips, Americans adjust to months of high gas prices
item.description: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.content:encoded: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: The U.S. adds 172,000 jobs. Many are in restaurants, bars and hotels
item.description: U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Former Fed chair Jerome Powell receives JFK Profile in Courage award
item.description: Former Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell was honored with the "Profile in Courage" award for helping to safeguard the central bank from political pressure from the White House.
item.content:encoded: Former Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell was honored with the "Profile in Courage" award for helping to safeguard the central bank from political pressure from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Here's how we're coping with high gas prices, according to Costco and Walmart
item.description: Sky-high gas prices have drivers going out of their way for discounts at the pump. Oil executives warn that even higher prices might be on the horizon.
item.content:encoded:
Sky-high gas prices have drivers going out of their way for discounts at the pump. Oil executives warn that even higher prices might be on the horizon.

(Image credit: Paul Sancya)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Despite Trump's pressure, high gas prices may keep Fed from cutting interest rates
item.description: Gas prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years. That's likely to make the Federal Reserve cautious about cutting interest rates, regardless of pressure from President Trump.
item.content:encoded: Gas prices pushed inflation to its highest level in almost three years. That's likely to make the Federal Reserve cautious about cutting interest rates, regardless of pressure from President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: As Trump embraces a gas tax holiday, there's a much bigger problem
item.description: President Trump's calls for a gas tax holiday raise a bigger issue: The gas tax is no longer covering the cost of the nation's highways anyway, and the problem will only get worse.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's calls for a gas tax holiday raise a bigger issue: The gas tax is no longer covering the cost of the nation's highways anyway, and the problem will only get worse.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: These swing voters are adding high gas prices into their political calculations
item.description: As part of NPR's Swing Shift project checking in with swing voters, these Americans are split on who is to blame for high gas prices but they all agree it has an impact on their personal finances.
item.content:encoded:
As part of NPR's Swing Shift project checking in with swing voters, these Americans are split on who is to blame for high gas prices but they all agree it has an impact on their personal finances.

(Image credit: Illustrations by Tara Anand)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Trump's proposed gas tax holiday could lead to another big problem for drivers
item.description: President Trump has proposed suspending the federal gas tax to lower the cost at the pump. But that could cost drivers another way -- potholes.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has proposed suspending the federal gas tax to lower the cost at the pump. But that could cost drivers another way -- potholes.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
